rationale: Both papers are in closely overlapping sales-force control/performance territory: the citing study positions itself as extending sales-force incentive/control research, and the cited paper is a core empirical study on sales force control systems affecting effort and percent-to-quota outcomes in new product contexts. Even though the raw match provides only a DOI (no in-text context), the construct overlap (control systems, effort, sales performance) makes this plausibly a theory/foundational source rather than an obvious incidental mention.
